title: Intern Talk & Connect
summary: Meet new people, share experiences, and gain insight into the internship journey in a supportive environment!
content:
On May 19, 2026, CODEC hosted an “Intern Talk and Connect” workshop focused on helping students prepare for future internship opportunities. Board members shared their experiences with applying for, preparing for, and navigating internships, offering practical advice and personal insights from their own professional journeys.

During the workshop, members learned strategies for building strong resumes, preparing for behavioral and technical interviews, and developing a professional online presence through LinkedIn. Speakers discussed approaches to interview preparation, such as the STAR method, resume review, company research, technical practice, and mock interviews. The event also covered ways students can maximize their internship experiences by taking initiative, documenting new skills and knowledge, and building meaningful professional relationships with teammates and mentors.

To help members practice professional networking and communication skills, attendees participated in an elevator pitch activity where they introduced themselves, their interests, and the value they hope to bring to future opportunities. The workshop concluded with an open Q&A session where members were able to ask board members questions about internships, career preparation, and professional growth. Through the event, students gained practical guidance and confidence to help them pursue upcoming Summer 2026 internships and future career opportunities.

title: How to Start a Personal Project
summary: Learn how to start your own personal project with the board!
content: On May 5, 2026, CODEC hosted a "How to Start a Personal Project" workshop. Various baord members with diverse expereinces shared their insights on how they started their personal projects, the tools they used, and worked hands-on with general members to help kick start each members' own project. Members worked in groups to brainstorm real problems and were given the opportunity to present their proposed solutions. During the workshop, we went through the process of brainstorming solutions to a problem and breaking down a project into its Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and core features. Members learned a guided approach towards solutions engineering by refining solutions and ideas into sets of features, narrowing down these features into an MVP (Minimum Viable Product) based off of scope and impact, and utilizing modern development tools and strategies to implement and deploy the final product.

title: Game Night Social
summary: Meet and play games with fellow members + Meet the board!
content: On April 21, 2026, CODEC hosted our first event for Spring Quarter: a Game Night Social. The event was designed to foster community and provide an opportunity for members to connect in a fun and relaxed environment. Attendees enjoyed a variety of games. The event also featured a 'Meet the Board' segment where members had the chance to interact with the CODEC leadership team and learn more about upcoming events. Overall, the Game Night Social was a great success, bringing together members from different backgrounds and creating lasting memories.
